description: P-401, P-402, P-403, P-501, P-602 all applied as defaults — saturation continues

Did NOT consume vendored doc-lint frontmatter parser despite it being available — kept whoodiff self-contained per its idea.md decision. Rationale: experiment 5 (whoorun) will explicitly test vendor-consumption pattern, so this experiment isolates the diff logic from upstream coupling.
Change codes as enum-like CODES object instead of inline strings. Stable contract for downstream tooling. Same shape as doc-lint error codes.
All 12 tests passed FIRST RUN. Zero deviations. Strong saturation evidence: with 9 prior patterns, the design space for a new tool of this size is heavily constrained — fewer wrong turns possible.

Array order matters in deepEqual (changed-value), even if elements are same. Alternative (order-insensitive) was considered: PAN frontmatter has 'allowed-tools' lists where order is meaningless, but 'phases' lists where order is meaningful. Chose strict (order-sensitive) as the safe default; users wanting order-insensitive can sort before diffing.
